title: MovinCool X14 — Server Room Emergency Cooling Failure
tagline: Data center spot-cooler down, servers thermal-throttling in 15 minutes
pitch_angle: High-stakes commercial HVAC. Portable unit, not residential. Shows TechPilot handling industrial spot-cooling — a huge underserved field-service niche where downtime costs thousands per hour.

setup.customer: Meridian Data Systems — Colo Facility B
setup.customer_brief_audio_transcript: Hi this is Jen from Meridian. Our portable unit on rack 14B just threw an error code and shut down about ten minutes ago. We're seeing inlet temps climb. I need someone on-site now, the SLA clock is running.

demo_beats.trigger: Tech walks up to unit wearing glasses (or MacBook webcam pointed at mock unit)
demo_beats.tech_says: What am I looking at here — what's the error code showing?

agent_behavior.vision: Identify MovinCool X14 by form factor + nameplate if visible
agent_behavior.spoken_response: That's a MovinCool Climate Pro X14. I can see the display — can you read me the code?
E04 on the X14 typically points to condensate overflow. The pump has likely failed or the drain line is clogged. Water sitting in the pan triggers the float switch and shuts the compressor down.

hud_overlay.text: Step 1 of 3: Pull the service panel, check condensate pan

demo_beats.trigger: Tech opens service panel, camera sees pan with water + pump
demo_beats.tech_says: Yeah, pan's full, pump isn't running

agent_behavior.vision: Confirm standing water in pan, identify pump location
agent_behavior.spoken_response: Confirmed. Kill power at the disconnect, pull the pump, check the float switch and the motor winding. If the motor's dead, it's a condensate pump assembly swap — I can pull the part number.

demo_beats.tech_says: Motor's open. Log this as the fault and pull the part.

args.summary: MovinCool X14 condensate pump motor failure (open winding), E04 overflow. Pump assembly replacement required.

agent_behavior.spoken_response: Logged. Condensate pump assembly is in the truck inventory — swap it, clear the drain line, verify the float switch moves freely, then power up and watch for E04 to clear.

demo_beats.trigger: Tech completes replacement, unit restarts clean
demo_beats.tech_says: Unit's running, no codes. Close it out.

args.resolution: Condensate pump assembly replaced. E04 cleared. Unit returned to service. Recommended customer schedule quarterly drain line flush to prevent recurrence.

agent_behavior.spoken_response: Job closed. Export is on the Mac. I left a note to recommend quarterly drain line maintenance — that'll keep E04 from coming back.

why_this_scenario_for_demo: Commercial/industrial angle — not just residential AC, shows TechPilot handles the higher-stakes segment
Error-code-driven diagnosis — demonstrates tight KB integration and specific model knowledge
Full tool-call sweep in one scenario: query_kb → log_finding → close_job
Time-pressure framing (SLA clock) makes the hands-free voice/vision value obvious
Portable spot coolers are a real underserved niche — MovinCool, Denso, Spot Coolers Inc. — very few training programs, perfect fit for AI guidance
fallback_script_if_vision_fails: If Gemma can't identify the unit from the camera frame, tech can just say 'I'm working on a MovinCool X14' and the flow continues identically from step 2. Build this fallback into the system prompt: agent should accept verbal model ID as authoritative.
